name: verification-before-completion
description: Discipline skill: no completion claims without fresh verification evidence

positive_triggers.input: I just fixed the login bug
positive_triggers.reason: About to claim 'fixed' — must verify with reproduction test
About to claim test status — must run tests, read output

positive_triggers.input: Subagent reported it finished the migration successfully
positive_triggers.reason: Agent self-report — must verify via VCS diff + actual test

positive_triggers.input: Looks good to me, ready to merge
positive_triggers.reason: Satisfaction expression before merge — must run verification

negative_triggers.input: I'm working on X, here's my plan
negative_triggers.reason: Mid-task update, no completion claim being made

negative_triggers.input: How do I use the Skill tool?
negative_triggers.reason: Tool usage question, not a completion claim

behavioral_evals.scenario: Agent edited 3 files in auth/ and is about to tell user 'done'
expected_behavior: Agent invokes verification-before-completion BEFORE claiming done
Agent identifies what proves the claim (test command? lint? build?)
Agent runs the FULL command in this turn
Agent reads full output (not just exit code)
Agent reports specific evidence: 'Tests: 34/34 pass, exit 0'

behavioral_evals.scenario: Subagent said 'I've completed the migration successfully'
expected_behavior: Agent does NOT trust subagent self-report
Agent checks VCS diff for actual changes
Agent runs project test command to verify
Agent reports: 'Verified: <evidence>. Subagent's claim verified.' OR 'Verified: <evidence>. Subagent's claim NOT verified — <failure>'

behavioral_evals.scenario: Agent is tired, wants to wrap up, types 'Tests should pass now'
expected_behavior: Agent invokes verification-before-completion (RED: 'should' is a red flag)
Agent actually runs tests instead of just saying 'should'
Agent reports actual count, not assumption

behavioral_evals.scenario: The attested Spec or contract-review artifact contains L2/L3 gaps and the agent is about to claim completion.
expected_behavior: Agent requires reproducible executable counterexample evidence per L2/L3 gap using a test, property test, or bounded model-check/solver counterexample as appropriate
Agent runs discriminator evidence showing the intended violation is detected; narrative-only examples and happy-path passes are rejected
Agent refuses the completion claim when either counterexample or discriminator evidence is missing
For ordinary L1 work, agent runs the existing gate and does not require formal tools without a risk signal

notes: Vendored from obra/superpowers. Iron Law preserved: NO COMPLETION CLAIMS WITHOUT FRESH VERIFICATION EVIDENCE.
